message:
harden(dep,progress): sanitize terminal control sequences in user output

Extends commit 764596f1's display sanitization to two paths it missed:

- src/cli/commands/dep.rs: route every issue ID, depends-on ID, and
  dependency-type string in dep_add / dep_remove / dep_list /
  dep_tree (mermaid label) through dep_display_text(), which calls
  the existing sanitize_terminal_inline helper. Without this, an
  attacker who can plant a crafted issue ID can inject ANSI escapes
  into "Added dependency:" / "Removed dependency:" / mermaid output.

- src/output/components/progress.rs: ProgressTracker previously
  passed the caller-supplied description straight into Console::print
  with markup-style brackets, which both opened a markup-injection
  path and let raw control characters through. Switch to building a
  rich_rust::Text via append_styled with an explicit Style — the
  description text never participates in markup parsing, and the
  string is sanitized first. Theme.success is captured at construction
  so the finish glyph keeps its accent style.

Tests:
- dep_display_and_mermaid_labels_escape_terminal_controls
- progress_prefix_sanitizes_description_without_markup_parsing
- progress_finish_message_sanitizes_description_without_markup_parsing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
